defects.title: Defect categories
defects.description: Here you can manage defect categories.
defects.formDescription: Use the form below to add a new defect category.
defects.description2: Below you can see the existing defect categories. You can edit or delete them as needed.

data.title: Data collection
data.paragraph1: We strive to provide the best diagnostic experience possible. To do this, we collect anonymized data about how you interact with our application.
data.paragraph2: By analyzing the interactions you make, we gain valuable insights into areas where our AI model may need to be improved. This allows us to continuously improve its accuracy and efficiency.
data.toggleLabel: I understand and agree to the data collection policy.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.paragraph1: Your feedback is essential to help us improve this application. By answering these questions, you help us understand what works well and what could be enhanced.
feedback.paragraph2: We appreciate your time and insights!
feedback.question1: 1. I would use the app again for this task.
feedback.question2: 2. The app was easy to use.
feedback.question3: 3. It was clear how to perform actions within the app.
feedback.question4: 4. I encountered issues or errors that slowed me down.
feedback.question5: 5. The app’s design was visually appealing and intuitive.
feedback.question6: 6. If I made a mistake, it was easy to fix.
feedback.question7: 7. I found the app unnecessarily complicated.
feedback.question8: 8. I think most people would learn to use this app very quickly.
feedback.question9: 9. I am confident that I knew how to use the app.
feedback.question10: 10. It took me a long time to learn how to start using the app.

reportProblem.title: Report problem
reportProblem.intro: Encountered an issue? Report it here and describe the problem in as much detail as possible.
reportProblem.thanksBody: We will review it and use it to improve the application in the future.

layoutDescription.description: PizzaScan is divided into four main areas: the top navigation bar, the left sidebar, the central workspace, and the right sidebar.

navigation.description: The navigation bar runs along the top of the application and contains the main tools:

features.ai: run automatic labeling on the currently open image.
features.aiDetection: detect individual pizzas and crop each one into a separate sub-image.
features.centerImage: Center image — fit the pizza into view after zooming or panning.
features.selectDefect: Defect selector — choose the defect or category for new labels. Can also be switched with number keys.
features.changeOpacity: Opacity — adjust the transparency of labels.
features.changeSize: Point size — adjust the size of label markers.
features.openProject: Open project — load a previously saved project.
features.saveProject: Save project — save the current state of the project.
features.exportProject: Export report — export inspection results to an XLSX file.
features.openSettings: Settings — change language, configure defect categories, and manage data collection preferences.
features.openDocumentation: Help — open this documentation or browse defect examples.

leftSidebar.description: The left sidebar lists all images in the current project. Images can be uploaded by clicking the Upload button or dragging and dropping files into the sidebar.

imageInteractions.click: Click an image to open it in the workspace.
imageInteractions.rightClick: Right-click an image to delete it, mark it as reviewed or unseen, or remove all its labels.
imageInteractions.subImages: When AI Detection is run, sub-images appear nested under the parent image. Click one to open it.

annotationList.header: Each image shows its label count. Expand it to see individual labels listed below.

mainImageArea.description: The central workspace displays the selected image along with all its labels. This is where you inspect the pizza and make label decisions.

annotation.add: Click on the image to add a new label at that position.
annotation.delete: Right-click a label to delete it.
annotation.details: Click a label to select it and view its details in the right sidebar.
annotation.move: Drag a label to reposition it.
annotation.copyPaste: Copy a label with CTRL+C and paste it with CTRL+V.

zoom.drag: Hold the left mouse button and drag to pan the image. Use the mouse wheel to zoom in and out.

subNavigation.description: When viewing a sub-image, arrow controls appear at the bottom of the workspace. Use them to move to the previous or next sub-image without returning to the sidebar.

rightSidebar.description: The right sidebar is split into three tabs: Project, Image, and Label.

projectOverview.description: Shows overall project statistics and lets you add notes or record the inspection outcome for the entire project.

imageOverview.description: Shows details about the currently open image. You can mark it as reviewed or unseen, delete all its labels, or add image-level notes.

annotationOverview.description: Shows details about the selected label. You can change its defect category and update any related fields.

manualTutorial.title: Manual Label
manualTutorial.intro: Manual Labeling lets you mark defect areas on pizza images by drawing labels. Each square is linked to a defect category represented by its color.
manualTutorial.newClass: Need a category that does not exist? Create your own in Settings.

steps.step1: Select an image from the left sidebar to open it in the workspace.
steps.step2: Choose a defect or category using the colored selector in the top bar. Number keys switch categories quickly.
steps.step3: Click on the pizza image to place a label.
steps.step4: Add as many labels as needed. One per defect area.
steps.step5: To edit a label, click it to select it, then use the Label tab on the right to change its category or update related fields.
steps.step6: To delete a label, right-click it, or use the remove option in the Label tab.

shortcuts.title: Key Shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Here are the key shortcuts you can use:

annotation.title: Label shortcuts
annotation.description: Shortcuts to select defect category can be changed in Settings.

aiAnnotation.title: AI Label
aiAnnotation.intro: AI Label automatically scans the current image and places labels around detected defect areas. You stay in control: review every suggestion and change anything you disagree with.

howItWorks.step1: Open an image in the workspace, then click the AI button in the top bar.
howItWorks.step2: AI places labels on the image. Each square has a color representing the detected defect type and a confidence level.
howItWorks.step3: Click any label to open a review popup. The popup shows the detected defect type and confidence.
howItWorks.step4: Click Accept to keep the suggestion as-is, or Decline to remove it.
howItWorks.step5: You can also freely move, resize, or change the defect category of any label, just like with manual labels.

confidence.description: Each AI suggestion comes with a confidence level: High, Medium, or Low. Low confidence means the AI was less certain. These labels deserve extra attention.

aiAnnotation.tip: AI Label is a starting point. It is faster than labeling from scratch, but you should always review the results. Incorrect suggestions can be declined or corrected.

aiDetection.title: AI Detection
aiDetection.intro: AI Detection finds individual pizzas within a single camera image and crops each one into a separate sub-image. This lets you inspect and label each pizza one by one.

howItWorks.step1: Open an image containing multiple pizzas, then click the AI Detection button in the top bar.
howItWorks.step2: Sub-images are created automatically and listed in the left sidebar under the original image.
howItWorks.step3: Click a sub-image to open it in the workspace, or use the arrows at the bottom of the workspace to navigate between sub-images.
howItWorks.step4: Label each sub-image manually or use the AI button to get automatic suggestions.
howItWorks.step5: Labels added to a sub-image also appear on the original image, so you always have the full picture.

aiDetection.tip: AI Detection is a starting point. You can remove incorrectly detected sub-images or manually handle any pizzas that were missed.

statistics.title: Statistics
statistics.totalMTDs: Total number of labeled cilia with microtubular defect
statistics.totalDA: Total number of labeled cilia with dynein arms
statistics.totalIDA: Total number of labeled cilia without inner dynein arms
statistics.totalODA: Total number of labeled cilia without outer dynein arms

defectExamples.title: Defect Examples
defectExamples.subtitle: Visual reference for each defect category used in pizza inspection.

welcome.title: Welcome to PizzaScan
welcome.description: Inspect pizzas one by one, work faster, stay organized, and cut down on repetitive work.

aiModel.title: AI assistance
aiModel.usage: PizzaScan can suggest initial labels and highlight regions to inspect, so you spend less time on repetitive pointing and clicking, and more time on review decisions.
aiModel.training: The model was trained on a diverse set of pizza inspection images.
aiModel.evolving: Suggestions are not guaranteed to be perfect: edge cases, unusual toppings, lighting, or occlusion can still be challenging. Treat AI output as a starting point: verify, correct when needed, and share feedback so the system can improve.

experience.title: Usage data & privacy
experience.dataCollection: To improve AI localization and label, we'd like your permission to collect anonymized usage data about how you interact with AI features, including how often you use them, whether you accept or modify suggestions, and how you work with generated results.
experience.insights: This data is used only to understand usage patterns and improve our AI features. You can change this preference later in Settings.
experience.agreement: I understand and agree to the collection of anonymized usage data as described above.

layoutTutorial.title: App layout
layoutTutorial.step1: Welcome to the tutorial! This tour highlights the main areas of PizzaScan: top bar, sidebars, and workspace.
layoutTutorial.step2: Use the AI button to automatically label the image, and the AI Detection button to detect pizzas on the image and crop them into individual images.
layoutTutorial.step3: Use the Center image button to fit the pizza in view, and the Undo button to reverse your last change.
layoutTutorial.step4: Use these controls to choose the defect or category for labels, and to adjust point size and opacity so marks are easy to see on the pizza.
layoutTutorial.step5: The left sidebar lists your images; expand an image to see its labels underneath.
layoutTutorial.step6: The center is your workspace: inspect the pizza, add or adjust labels, and zoom or pan to see details.
layoutTutorial.step7: The right sidebar has Project, Image, and Label tabs for summaries and details. Click a tab to switch.

manualAnnotationTutorial.title: Manual labeling
manualAnnotationTutorial.step1: This short guide walks you through manual labeling on pizza images.
manualAnnotationTutorial.step2: Select an image in the left sidebar to open it.
manualAnnotationTutorial.step3: Pick a defect or category in the top bar (colored selector).
manualAnnotationTutorial.step4: Click on the pizza image to add a label.
manualAnnotationTutorial.step5: You can add more than one label on the same pizza image.
manualAnnotationTutorial.step6: To edit a label, select it and use the Label tab on the right to change the defect category and related fields.
manualAnnotationTutorial.step7: To delete a label, right-click it, or remove it in the Label tab on the right.

aiDetectionTutorial.title: AI Detection
aiDetectionTutorial.step1: This short guide walks you through AI Detection. AI finds individual pizzas on the image and crops each one into a separate sub-image.
aiDetectionTutorial.step2: Sub-images appear in the left sidebar, listed under the original image. Click one to open it in the workspace.
aiDetectionTutorial.step3: Label each sub-image just like a regular image. Your labels are linked to that sub-image.
aiDetectionTutorial.step4: Any label you add to a sub-image also appears on the main image. You always have the full picture.
aiDetectionTutorial.step5: You can label sub-images manually, or let AI do it automatically using the AI button.

AItutorial.title: AI Labeling
AItutorial.step1: This short guide walks you through reviewing AI-suggested labels on pizza images. You stay in control. Only change what needs changing.
AItutorial.step2: When you run AI Labeling, AI suggests a defect category for each pizza on the image.
AItutorial.step3: Each label is color-coded by confidence: green is high, orange is medium, and red means the suggestion most likely needs a change.
AItutorial.step4: Click a label to open a review panel. Change the defect category if needed, then press Accept to keep it or Decline to remove it. Everything else stays as AI suggested.
AItutorial.step5: To edit more details, select the label and use the Label tab on the right sidebar.

0.title: Step 1: Open the Image
0.description: Select the image you want to label by clicking on it in the left sidebar.

1.title: Step 2: Choose a Defect Category
1.description: In the navigation bar, select a defect category using the colored selector. You can also switch categories using the numeric keys.

2.title: Step 3: Create Labels
2.description: Click on the pizza image to add a label. You can place multiple labels on the same image.

3.title: Step 4: Review Your Labels
3.description: Each label is shown as a colored square. Click on it to select it and review or confirm its defect category in the right sidebar.

4.title: Need to Edit an Label?
4.description: Click on a label, then open the Label tab in the right sidebar to change the defect category or any related details.

5.title: Need to Delete an Label?
5.description: Right-click a label to delete it directly, or select it and use the Label tab in the right sidebar.

status.export: Report was successfully exported and project was saved
status.zoom: Zoom in and out of image with mouse wheel
status.copy: Label copied, press CTRL+V to paste
